today_focus.title: OpenAI："We used to say build the MVP. Now you should build the system that builds the MVP first."

today_focus.social_original_text:
"We used to say build the MVP. Now you should build the system that builds the MVP first."

Here's my new episode with @ryancarson where he shared how he runs his startup solo with AI agents:

✅ OpenClaw as his AI chief of staff to triage emails, book meetings, and do sales outreach

✅ Codex and Devin as his AI eng team to ship features while he sleeps

Some quotes from Ryan:

"Spend a lot of time upfront setting up your skills + documentation. Then you've suddenly unlocked the work of 10 people."

"Treat your agent like a real employee. Give it a real email address, calendar access, and GitHub account."

"Pay a designer to set up your design system and brand. After that, you can use AI to generate on-brand assets."

📌 Watch now: https://t.co/Slc5cEkFGQ

Thanks to our sponsors:

@WisprFlow: Don't type, just speak https://t.co/oqHJ8bN3ll

@linear: The AI agent platform for modern teams https://t.co/yFylGfkenJ

today_focus.title: CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI.
today_focus.brief: CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI。先放在观察位，等更多证据出现后再判断它的行业分量。
today_focus.social_original_text:
CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI.

So when they play with AI, they see the happy path results, often not considering the next 10 or 20 things that have to happen to get sustainable results from agents.

“Look I made this awesome product prototype”. Yes but you didn’t have to review the code before it went into production and fix a bunch of issues.

“Look I generated a contract”. Yes but you didn’t verify all the terms before it goes out to the counterparty and didn’t have to wire up all the past contracts to work with.

The best thing you can do as a CEO is to use AI a *ton* to figure out the real implications of agents in the enterprise, and come out the other side with an appreciation for both the upside and the real work that goes into them.
evidence_titles: CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI.
evidence_summaries:
CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI.

So when they play with AI, they see the happy path results, often not considering the next 10 or 20 things that have to happen to get sustainable results from agents.

“Look I made this awesome product prototype”. Yes but you didn’t have to review the code before it went into production and fix a bunch of issues.

“Look I generated a contract”. Yes but you didn’t verify all the terms before it goes out to the counterparty and didn’t have to wire up all the past contracts to work with.

The best thing you can do as a CEO is to use AI a *ton* to figure out the real implications of agents in the enterprise, and come out the other side with an appreciation for both the upside and the real work that goes into them.

worth_tracking.title: CEOs feel AI FOMO, but many of them and their lieutenants have gotten used to arms-length leadership and lack the muscle to get hands-on with AI.
worth_tracking.brief: CEOs feel AI FOMO, but many of them and their lieutenants have gotten used to arms-length leadership and lack the muscle to get hands-on with AI。先放在观察位，等更多证据出现后再判断它的行业分量。
worth_tracking.social_original_text:
CEOs feel AI FOMO, but many of them and their lieutenants have gotten used to arms-length leadership and lack the muscle to get hands-on with AI.

So they set sweeping, imprecise AI mandates for their company.

This leads to performative, low-effort demos from employees trying to meet the coarse goals.

Next thing you know it’s been two years with no real progress and a startup with hands-on leadership is disrupting you.
evidence_titles: CEOs feel AI FOMO, but many of them and their lieutenants have gotten used to arms-length leadership and lack the muscle to get hands-on with AI.
evidence_summaries:
CEOs feel AI FOMO, but many of them and their lieutenants have gotten used to arms-length leadership and lack the muscle to get hands-on with AI.

So they set sweeping, imprecise AI mandates for their company.

This leads to performative, low-effort demos from employees trying to meet the coarse goals.

Next thing you know it’s been two years with no real progress and a startup with hands-on leadership is disrupting you.

items.title: Introduction
items.takeaway_zh: 《Introduction》探索多模态理解、生成或跨模态推理的新方法。
items.why_it_matters_zh: 它可能影响 Agent 训练、推理策略或任务自动化能力。

items.title: 1 Images generated by Lens at 1440 resolution. Section provides more visualizations.

items.takeaway_zh: 《1 Images generated by Lens at 1440 resolution. Section provides more visualizations.》探索多模态理解、生成或跨模态推理的新方法。
items.why_it_matters_zh: 它指向多模态模型的能力边界和产品化机会。

items.title: Rethinking Cross-Layer Information Routing in Diffusion Transformers

items.why_it_matters_zh: 它可能影响 Agent 训练、推理策略或任务自动化能力。

items.title: SciAtlas: A Large-Scale Knowledge Graph for Automated Scientific Research
items.title_zh: SciAtlas: A Large-Scale Knowledge Graph for Automated Scientific Research
items.takeaway_zh: 《SciAtlas: A Large-Scale Knowledge Graph for Automated Scientific Research》围绕推理过程建模，探索更强的递归式问题求解。
items.why_it_matters_zh: 它可能影响 Agent 训练、推理策略或任务自动化能力。

"I think if we froze the models that we have right now and you really worked on the harness and maybe we also spent more time training with a great harness, I think people would really feel the AGI in every single domain" - @yanndubs of @OpenAI https://t.co/amFrnChXkC https://t.co/nGv05BVzMI
evidence_titles: Is AGI already here?
evidence_summaries:

items.title: CEOs feel AI FOMO, but many of them and their lieutenants have gotten used to arms-length leadership and lack the muscle to get hands-on with AI.
items.brief: CEOs feel AI FOMO, but many of them and their lieutenants have gotten used to arms-length leadership and lack the muscle to get hands-on with AI。先放在观察位，等更多证据出现后再判断它的行业分量。
items.social_original_text:
CEOs feel AI FOMO, but many of them and their lieutenants have gotten used to arms-length leadership and lack the muscle to get hands-on with AI.

So they set sweeping, imprecise AI mandates for their company.

This leads to performative, low-effort demos from employees trying to meet the coarse goals.

Next thing you know it’s been two years with no real progress and a startup with hands-on leadership is disrupting you.
evidence_titles: CEOs feel AI FOMO, but many of them and their lieutenants have gotten used to arms-length leadership and lack the muscle to get hands-on with AI.
evidence_summaries:
CEOs feel AI FOMO, but many of them and their lieutenants have gotten used to arms-length leadership and lack the muscle to get hands-on with AI.

So they set sweeping, imprecise AI mandates for their company.

This leads to performative, low-effort demos from employees trying to meet the coarse goals.

Next thing you know it’s been two years with no real progress and a startup with hands-on leadership is disrupting you.
